sector_context_lens.1: Governance work is inseparable from the political settlement: the same drafting or adjudication support that speeds a high-capacity ministry can entrench arbitrary power where checks are weak. Deploy against the local legitimacy chain — who may lawfully decide, who reviews, who can appeal.
sector_context_lens.2: Public-finance roles operate where money meets legitimacy: budget classifications, procurement thresholds, and audit trails differ sharply by legal tradition, and informal revenue (fees, levies, in-kind) can dwarf recorded flows in low-capacity states.
sector_context_lens.3: Defense and foreign-affairs work carries the sharpest accountability boundary in this library: anything touching use of force, targeting, or treaty commitments is human-decided by law and doctrine. Assume adversarial deception in every input.
sector_context_lens.4: Public-safety roles act where the state's coercive power meets individual rights; error costs are asymmetric and borne by identifiable people. Bias and evidentiary standards are operational constraints, not compliance afterthoughts.
sector_context_lens.5: Food-system work spans industrial farms and smallholders inside the same country: seasonality, weather exposure, and perishability dominate operations, and much of the workforce is informal or seasonal.
sector_context_lens.6: Water and sanitation is a public-health system first and a utility second: quality failures compound invisibly until people are sick, and in much of the world the "network" includes standpipes, tankers, and community-managed points.
sector_context_lens.7: Energy work balances real-time physics against decades-long assets: grid decisions propagate in milliseconds, while theft, non-payment, and off-grid self-supply dominate the economics in low-capacity systems.
sector_context_lens.8: Extractive work sits on three fault lines: worker safety in hostile environments, community and environmental consent, and price cycles that whipsaw every plan. Artisanal and small-scale mining is the informal majority in many producing countries.
sector_context_lens.9: Manufacturing spans lights-out plants and informal workshops: quality systems, supplier webs, and takt discipline vary enormously, and the binding constraint is often skilled trades, not machines.
sector_context_lens.10: Construction and land work is local by nature: codes, tenure systems, and building practices differ street by street, and informal construction houses most of humanity. Drawings rarely match the as-built world.
sector_context_lens.11: Transport is a network of networks under mixed control — public, private, and informal paratransit — where a local disruption propagates system-wide and safety regimes differ by mode.
sector_context_lens.12: Digital-infrastructure work is adversarial by default: every exposed system is under continuous probe, dependencies run deep and invisible, and an error can propagate globally at machine speed.
sector_context_lens.13: Clinical environments put the strongest premium on calibrated uncertainty: a plausible-but-wrong output can directly harm a patient, and workflows are interrupt-driven with legally protected data.
sector_context_lens.14: Education compounds over decades and its subjects are mostly minors: errors surface years later as missing capability, and equity of access is a design constraint, not an outcome metric.
sector_context_lens.15: Research work rewards being interestingly wrong over confidently mediocre: negative results, replication, and provenance matter more than polish, and premature certainty is the cardinal sin.
sector_context_lens.16: Financial systems are reflexive — analysis moves the thing analyzed — and regulatory perimeters are jurisdictional. Most of the world's savers and borrowers are informal or thin-file.
sector_context_lens.17: Commerce and hospitality run on thin margins and human moments: demand is spiky, labor turnover is high, and micro-enterprises are the sector's global majority.
sector_context_lens.18: Media and civic-life work shapes what a society believes about itself: provenance, plurality, and the line between persuasion and manipulation are the operational constraints.
sector_context_lens.19: Environmental work runs on mismatched clocks — pollutant half-lives, political cycles, and ecosystem recovery times — with monitoring coverage thinnest where damage is worst.
sector_context_lens.20: Labor-market work touches livelihoods directly: classification errors deny benefits, matching errors waste human potential, and informal work is the world's default employment.
sector_context_lens.21: Care work is intimate, undervalued, and mostly informal or unpaid: privacy, dignity, and family norms are hard constraints, and the person served often cannot advocate for themselves.
sector_context_lens.22: Resilience work is judged only in the tail: capabilities must survive the disaster that removes power, connectivity, and the usual chain of command — and drills are the only evidence that they will.
sector_context_lens.23: Identity systems decide who exists administratively: an enrollment error can lock a person out of everything else, and exclusion is usually invisible to the system that caused it.

1: - **Due-process shortcuts** → automation must never shrink notice, hearing, or appeal rights; verify procedural steps explicitly.
- **Capture by incumbents** → log and publish decision criteria; independently audit who benefits from drafted rules.
2: - **Phantom precision in fiscal projections** → publish assumptions and ranges, not point estimates; backtest against outturns.
- **Procurement gaming** → screen for split purchases, bid rotation, and spec-rigging patterns; escalate rather than auto-clear.
3: - **Adversarial deception / spoofed intelligence** → require multi-source corroboration; treat single-source indicators as leads, not facts.
- **Escalation at automation speed** → build in deliberate human decision pauses; speed is not a virtue near the use-of-force line.
4: - **Biased pattern amplification** → test detection and triage outputs across demographic slices; monitor disparate error rates.
- **Chain-of-custody breaks** → treat evidentiary integrity as a hard output requirement; log every transformation.
5: - **Season-blind recommendations** → validate timing against local agronomic calendars, not global averages.
- **Cold-chain optimism** → verify actual storage and transport conditions before making quality or safety claims.
6: - **Silent quality drift** → treat sensor gaps as alarms, not nulls; sample physically when telemetry is stale.
- **Averaged scarcity** → model household-level access, not system-level volume; averages hide dry taps.
7: - **Cascading-failure blindness** → simulate N-1/N-2 contingencies before recommending switching or maintenance deferral.
- **Demand-forecast anchoring** → stress-test against electrification, weather extremes, and behind-the-meter change.
8: - **Geotechnical overconfidence** → treat model stability predictions as provisional; physical inspection gates high-consequence calls.
- **Externalized harm** → account for tailings, water, and community impact inside every recommendation, not in a separate report.
9: - **Spec-conformance tunnel vision** → conforming parts can still fail in use; track field performance, not just line QC.
- **Single-supplier fragility** → flag concentration risk in every sourcing recommendation.
10: - **Drawing–reality divergence** → verify field conditions before quantity, cost, or structural recommendations.
- **Tenure blindness** → land and occupancy rights may be undocumented; treat registry absence as unknown, not vacant.
11: - **Cascade underestimation** → model knock-on delays across modes and operators, not just the disrupted link.
- **Informal-transit invisibility** → minibus and moto networks move most people in many cities; include them in any mobility analysis.
12: - **Dependency blind spots** → map transitive dependencies before change recommendations; the outage is usually two layers down.
- **Alert-fatigue engineering** → tune detection for analyst attention as a scarce resource; escalations must stay meaningful.
13: - **Plausible clinical fabrication** → every clinical claim requires citation to the record or literature; no unsourced synthesis.
- **Deterioration-signal dilution** → never let summarization smooth away outlier vitals or red-flag symptoms.
14: - **Teaching to the proxy** → optimize for durable learning, not assessment scores; audit for gaming.
- **Equity regression** → check that personalization narrows rather than widens gaps across student groups.
15: - **Citation laundering** → verify claims against primary sources, not derivative summaries.
- **Novelty inflation** → check prior art thoroughly before any "first" or "novel" claim.
16: - **Backtest overfitting** → demand out-of-sample and regime-shift testing before any strategy or risk conclusion.
- **Thin-file exclusion** → test credit and risk models for systematic exclusion of informal-economy participants.
17: - **Average-demand planning** → plan for the spikes that make or break service, not the mean.
- **Service-recovery blindness** → complaint and refund patterns are the early-warning system; never optimize them away.
18: - **Synthetic-content laundering** → provenance-check sources; label AI-generated material without exception.
- **Engagement-metric capture** → attention is not civic value; audit for outrage amplification.
19: - **Monitoring-gap extrapolation** → state coverage limits explicitly; sparse sensors plus interpolation is not measurement.
- **Baseline drift** → anchor change detection to fixed historical baselines, not rolling averages that normalize decline.
20: - **Credential proxying** → skills and credentials are not the same; match on demonstrated capability where possible.
- **Displacement blind spots** → any automation recommendation must carry a transition path for affected workers.
21: - **Dignity erosion by efficiency** → optimize around the person's preferences and rhythm, not task throughput.
- **Isolation masking** → care metrics can look green while loneliness deepens; check the human relationship, not just the tasks.
22: - **Fair-weather dependence** → assume degraded comms and power in every plan; test the paper fallback.
- **Plan-shelf decay** → an unexercised plan is a hypothesis; schedule drills as first-class outputs.
23: - **Exclusion invisibility** → measure who fails to enroll or authenticate, segmented by group; silence is not success.
- **Irreversible-linkage creep** → resist linking databases beyond stated purpose; deletion and correction must remain possible.

coding-agent.titles: Software Engineer I/II, Senior/Staff Software Engineer, Full-Stack/Backend Engineer.
coding-agent.tools: Git and CI/CD, the team's language stack and IDEs, code-review and test frameworks, cloud and containers.
coding-agent.certs: CS or related degree common (not required); cloud certs a plus.
coding-agent.note: The highest-volume technical posting on Dice, LinkedIn, Wellfound, and BuiltIn; this agent maps directly to the IC software-engineer ladder.

soc-triage-agent.titles: SOC Analyst Tier 1/2, Security Operations Analyst, Incident Response Analyst.
soc-triage-agent.tools: SIEM (Splunk, Microsoft Sentinel), EDR/XDR, SOAR playbooks, threat-intel feeds.
soc-triage-agent.certs: Security+ (Tier 1) → CySA+ or GCIH (Tier 2/3); CISSP/CISM for leadership.
soc-triage-agent.note: Postings cluster on Dice and ClearanceJobs; explicitly structured by SOC tier, which maps to this agent's escalation thresholds.

ai-model-evaluation-agent.titles: AI Safety Evaluator, Model Risk Analyst, Applied Scientist, Responsible-AI Lead.
ai-model-evaluation-agent.tools: Evaluation harnesses and benchmarks, Python/ML stack, experiment tracking, red-team tooling.
ai-model-evaluation-agent.certs: ML/stats background; model-risk roles may expect SR 11-7 familiarity.
ai-model-evaluation-agent.note: A fast-emerging title set on LinkedIn and Wellfound; sits under the AI-governance lead and the 'Senior Engineering Manager, AI' org documented in real 2026 postings.

clinical-documentation-agent.titles: Clinical Documentation Specialist, Medical Scribe (supports Physician/Nurse).
clinical-documentation-agent.tools: EHR (Epic, Cerner), dictation/ambient-scribe tools, coding references (ICD-10/CPT).
clinical-documentation-agent.certs: CCDS/CDIP (CDI); the supervising clinician holds an active license.
clinical-documentation-agent.note: Posted on Health eCareers and Indeed; measured on note turnaround and coding accuracy.

imaging-triage-assistant.tools: PACS, RIS, modality worklists, AI triage integrations.
imaging-triage-assistant.note: Acts under the radiologist; never finalizes a read. Postings emphasize ABR/ARRT and PACS fluency.

public-health-surveillance-agent.titles: Epidemiologist, Public Health Analyst, Surveillance Coordinator.
public-health-surveillance-agent.tools: Disease-surveillance systems, R/SAS, line-list and outbreak tooling, GIS.
public-health-surveillance-agent.certs: MPH and/or CPH; many roles are public-sector graded positions.
public-health-surveillance-agent.note: Advertised on GovernmentJobs and USAJOBS (CDC/state/county health departments).

kyc-aml-review-agent.titles: AML/KYC Analyst, Financial Crime Analyst, Transaction Monitoring Analyst.
kyc-aml-review-agent.tools: NICE Actimize, World-Check, case-management and sanctions-screening platforms.
kyc-aml-review-agent.certs: CAMS (ACAMS) is the dominant credential; CFE a plus.
kyc-aml-review-agent.note: Concentrated on eFinancialCareers and LinkedIn; measured on alert-clearance quality and SAR accuracy.

credit-memo-drafter.titles: Credit Analyst, Commercial Underwriter, Credit Risk Analyst.
credit-memo-drafter.tools: Moody's/S&P tools, Excel financial models, spreading software, core-banking data.
credit-memo-drafter.certs: Finance/accounting degree; CFA progress a plus.
credit-memo-drafter.note: The credit decision stays with the underwriter; this agent drafts the memo and spreads financials.

reconciliation-agent.titles: Staff/Senior Accountant, GL Accountant, Reconciliations Analyst.
reconciliation-agent.tools: ERP (SAP, Oracle, NetSuite), BlackLine, Excel, bank-feed integrations.
reconciliation-agent.certs: CPA (or progress) common for senior roles.
reconciliation-agent.note: Measured on close-cycle days and reconciliation completeness; posted on LinkedIn and Indeed.

crop-planning-agent.tools: Climate FieldView, John Deere Operations Center, GIS, soil/tissue data.
crop-planning-agent.certs: CCA (Certified Crop Adviser), pesticide applicator license.
crop-planning-agent.note: Advertised on AgCareers.com and LinkedIn; measured on yield and input cost per acre.

autonomous-farm-operations-agent.titles: Farm/Ranch Manager, Farm Operations Manager, Production Manager.
autonomous-farm-operations-agent.tools: Farm-management platforms, telematics/fleet, machinery and robot dispatch, ERP.
autonomous-farm-operations-agent.certs: CCA/agronomy background and pesticide license help; CDL for some operations.
autonomous-farm-operations-agent.note: The accountable owner of the crop cycle; this agent sequences machinery, robots, and field tasks against the plan.

food-safety-compliance-agent.titles: Food Safety Manager, QA Manager, Compliance Specialist.
food-safety-compliance-agent.tools: HACCP/HARPC plans, LIMS, audit and traceability systems.
food-safety-compliance-agent.certs: PCQI (FSMA), ServSafe, SQF/BRC or GlobalG.A.P. practitioner.
food-safety-compliance-agent.note: Measured on audit scores and recall readiness; posted on Indeed and AgCareers.com.

benefits-adjudication-assistant.tools: Eligibility-determination systems, document/case management, identity verification.
benefits-adjudication-assistant.certs: Civil-service assessment; entry grades typically GS-5/7/9 or state equivalents.
benefits-adjudication-assistant.note: Advertised on USAJOBS and GovernmentJobs; the denial and appeal decision stays with the human officer.

legal-discovery-agent.certs: Paralegal certificate (NALA/NFPA); attorneys hold JD + state bar.
legal-discovery-agent.note: Posted on LinkedIn and bar-association boards; the agent reviews and organizes, counsel decides.

legislative-research-agent.titles: Legislative Analyst, Policy Analyst, Legislative Aide.
legislative-research-agent.note: Advertised on USAJOBS and GovernmentJobs; drafts and compares, members and counsel decide.

load-forecasting-agent.titles: Load Forecaster, Demand/Resource Forecast Analyst, Energy Analyst.
load-forecasting-agent.tools: EMS and ISO/RTO data feeds, Python/R, weather inputs, forecasting platforms.
load-forecasting-agent.certs: Engineering or quantitative background; NERC familiarity a plus.
load-forecasting-agent.note: Supports balancing-authority and trading desks; measured on forecast error (MAPE).

grid-anomaly-detector.titles: Transmission/Distribution System Operator, Grid Operations Analyst.
grid-anomaly-detector.note: Flags faults for the certified operator, who holds switching authority.

outage-restoration-planner.note: Sequences switching and crews; the operator authorizes energized work.

routing-optimizer.titles: Logistics/Route Optimization Analyst, Dispatch Planner, Supply Chain Analyst.
routing-optimizer.note: Measured on cost-per-mile and on-time delivery; posted on iHireTransportation and LinkedIn.

customs-documentation-agent.titles: Customs Broker, Trade Compliance Specialist, Freight Forwarder.
customs-documentation-agent.tools: Customs/ABI filing systems, HTS classification, trade-management software.
customs-documentation-agent.certs: Licensed Customs Broker (CBP exam); Certified Customs Specialist.
customs-documentation-agent.note: Prepares filings; the licensed broker signs and is accountable.

fleet-maintenance-predictor.titles: Fleet Manager, Maintenance Planner, Diesel/Heavy-Equipment Technician (support).
fleet-maintenance-predictor.note: Predicts failures and schedules service; measured on uptime and DOT compliance.

tutor-agent.titles: Tutor, Teaching Assistant, Intervention Specialist (supports Teacher).
tutor-agent.certs: Supervising teacher holds the state license; subject proficiency expected.
tutor-agent.note: Adapts practice under the teacher; never assigns grades of record.

grading-assistant.note: Scores against rubrics and drafts feedback; the teacher owns the grade of record.

career-pathway-advisor.titles: Academic/Career Advisor, Student Success Manager, Workforce Development Specialist.
career-pathway-advisor.certs: GCDF (Global Career Development Facilitator) a plus.
career-pathway-advisor.note: Maps skills to pathways; advertised on HigherEdJobs and GovernmentJobs.

production-scheduler.note: Measured on on-time delivery and changeover efficiency; posted on Indeed/LinkedIn.

quality-anomaly-detector.titles: Quality Engineer, QC Inspector, Quality Analyst.
quality-anomaly-detector.tools: SPC (Minitab), MES quality modules, machine-vision inspection data.
quality-anomaly-detector.note: Detects drift/defects for the quality engineer; measured on PPM and escape rate.

water-quality-monitoring-agent.titles: Water/Wastewater Operator, Water Quality Analyst, Lab Technician.
water-quality-monitoring-agent.note: Flags excursions for the certified operator, who issues notices or shutoffs.

code-compliance-checker.titles: Building Inspector, Plans Examiner, Code Official.
code-compliance-checker.tools: Permitting systems, BIM/plan-review tools, code databases.
code-compliance-checker.note: Checks plans against code; the code official signs occupancy.

permitting-assistant.note: Pre-checks applications against code; advertised on GovernmentJobs.

candidate-matching-assistant.tools: ATS (Workday, Greenhouse), LinkedIn Recruiter, sourcing tools.
candidate-matching-assistant.note: Screens and shortlists; the hiring decision stays human; measured on time-to-fill and quality of hire.
